reasoning: Based on betting odds and prediction markets, the Philadelphia Eagles are currently favorites to win Super Bowl LX, priced between +600 and +700 at major sportsbooks. These odds reflect an implied probability of 12.5-14.3%, which corresponds to a true probability of ~10-12% once the sportsbook vig is removed. The Polymarket estimate was previously 12%. The Eagles have mostly preserved their offensive core from their Super Bowl LIX victory, though their defense has lost some established talent (such as Darius Slay, C.J. Gardner-Johnson, Josh Sweat, Brandon Graham) which may make a repeat slightly more challenging. As of early August 2025, there have been no major, season-ending injuries to key stars like Jalen Hurts or Saquon Barkley. However, they are currently managing typical training camp injuries, including a hamstring issue for WR A.J. Brown (who missed practice on August 3) and injuries to defensive players like Nakobe Dean (on PUP). Since training camp bumps are usually expected and mostly priced in, we adjust the baseline very marginally downwards to balance the early camp injuries, landing my estimate at a highly confident 11%.
